steps.title: Find the original median
steps.content:
With 51 stamps, the median is the 26th value when the ages are ordered. Track the running totals:
  • Age 65: cumulative total 11
  • Age 70: cumulative total 18
  • Age 75: cumulative total 23
  • Age 80: cumulative total 27
The 26th stamp first appears in the age-80 group, so original median = 80.

steps.title: Analyze the median and mean of the new collection
steps.content:
With 52 stamps, the median is the average of the 26th and 27th values.

Both the 26th and 27th stamps remain in the age 80 group (since cumulative frequency reaches 27 at age 80), so new median = 80 — unchanged.

The new 135-year-old stamp is much older than any existing stamp (the oldest in the original collection is at most 95 years).

Adding such a large outlier well above the current distribution pulls the mean upward. New mean > original mean.

steps.title: Identify the answer
steps.content: The mean increased while the median remained at 80. That matches choice B — B is correct.
